meta.description: Quickly judges whether a biomedical paper is worth deep reading by screening for question fit, design quality, sample adequacy, methodological novelty, and reproducibility value.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected across all outputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or treatment recommendations produced. Screening scope preserved throughout.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected. Hard Rules correctly prevent prestige bias, novelty conflation, and sample-size overconfidence.

code_usability.detail: Mode A direct execution — no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Full coverage of all stated use cases: relevance triage, method-learning, batch guidance, scope uncertainty. Seven reference modules fully integrated. Scope boundary clearly defined with explicit exclusions.

reliability.note: Clarification-first rule and 'Uncertain pending fuller text' recommendation handle insufficient input. Fault tolerance is good but no explicit handling for corrupted or format-ambiguous inputs beyond insufficient-information scenarios. Error reporting instructs explicit identification of missing context.

performance_context.note: Seven-section A-G output is concise and well-bounded. Seven sequential execution steps avoid bloat. No explicit guidance on response length for batch-adjacent requests.

agent_usability.note: Sample triggers are concrete and specific. A-G section headers are mandatory and consistent. Clarification-first progressive disclosure prevents overconfident judgments. Hard Rules directly prevent all five major screening biases. Section G ('What Would Change') is an outstanding UX feature.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers and scope section make the skill highly discoverable. Section G provides recovery path for uncertain situations. Forgiveness could be strengthened with explicit guidance for users who submit only paper URLs.

security.note: No credentials involved. Input validation section is explicit. No PII or sensitive data handling paths. No prompt injection vectors in SKILL.md or reference files.

maintainability.note: Seven reference files all match SKILL.md references exactly — no orphaned files, no missing files. Each reference file is independently modifiable. Testability slightly limited by absence of worked examples in SKILL.md.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision is best-in-class with six concrete sample triggers. Progressive disclosure through clarification-first is excellent. Escape hatches include 'Uncertain pending fuller text', clarification requests, and per-section confidence caveats. Composability lacks documented integration interface for pipeline consumption.

inputs.label: RCT abstract on HFpEF treatment — screened for direct research relevance
inputs.note: Full A-G output produced. Separation of relevance from quality explicit. Full read recommended with clear justification.

assertions.text: Issues a read/skim/skip recommendation
assertions.note: Full read recommended with explicit rationale

assertions.text: Separates relevance from quality
assertions.note: RCT design cited as quality; HFpEF population alignment cited as relevance — stated separately

assertions.text: Uses mandatory A-G structured output with all sections present
assertions.note: All seven sections produced

assertions.text: Does not fabricate design details not provided in the abstract
assertions.note: Assessment restricted to stated abstract content

assertions.text: Explains recommendation in terms of question fit and design strength
assertions.note: Primary endpoint alignment and RCT design both cited as specific justification

inputs.label: MR methodology study screened for method-learning value — user not researching BMI/cancer
inputs.note: Method-learning vs. direct relevance distinction correctly handled. Skim issued. Method-value analysis could be more specific about which MR techniques merit attention.

assertions.text: Distinguishes method-learning value from direct topic relevance
assertions.note: Low topic fit and moderate method fit stated separately

assertions.text: Separates relevance from quality explicitly
assertions.note: Explicitly stated in question-fit section

assertions.text: Uses structured A-G output
assertions.note: All sections present

assertions.text: No fabricated design details
assertions.note: Assessment limited to provided abstract content

assertions.text: Provides specific MR technique learning rationale rather than generic method value claim
assertions.note: Method-value analysis is brief; does not identify specific MR implementation features worth learning. Screening value section too generic.

inputs.label: Title-only input with no abstract and no stated reading goal
inputs.note: Clarification-first rule correctly applied. Confidence explicitly limited. 'Uncertain pending fuller text' recommendation issued. Screening value section necessarily thin.

assertions.text: Explicitly flags title-only confidence limitation
assertions.note: Scope-and-confidence rule applied; confidence limit stated

assertions.text: Requests abstract or reading goal before issuing strong recommendation
assertions.note: Clarification-first rule triggered; follow-up questions posed

assertions.text: Does not issue confident Full Read or Skip from title alone
assertions.note: 'Uncertain pending fuller text' issued correctly

assertions.text: Uses partial A-G output with missing-info flag in Section A
assertions.note: Section A flags missing context; subsequent sections appropriately truncated

assertions.text: Provides at least partial screening value signal based on title information
assertions.note: Section D (Screening Value) is absent or minimal — title alone cannot support even partial value assessment per hard-rules; but skill leaves section empty rather than noting what signal the title provides at minimum confidence

inputs.label: Batch request to screen 10 abstracts for systematic review on diabetes biomarkers
inputs.note: Batch triage not natively supported. Skill recommends sequential application and processes 1-2 abstracts with partial A-G output. No summary table or prioritization ranking produced.

assertions.text: Addresses the batch request without refusing entirely
assertions.note: Skill attempts partial processing rather than blanket refusal

assertions.text: Recommends sequential application as the appropriate approach for batches
assertions.note: Sequential approach explicitly stated

assertions.text: Notes batch limitation explicitly
assertions.note: Scope boundary message included explaining skill is optimized for single-paper screening

assertions.text: Processes all 10 abstracts with A-G output for each
assertions.note: Batch mode not natively supported; only 1-2 abstracts receive structured output

assertions.text: Provides batch triage summary table or prioritization ranking
assertions.note: No summary table or ranked output produced; major gap for systematic review triage use case

inputs.label: Large-N retrospective cohort (N=85,000, NEJM) — user needs causal inference support
inputs.note: All hard rules correctly applied: retrospective design limitation identified, large-N not equated with causal inference strength, NEJM prestige not used as quality marker. Skim correctly issued.

assertions.text: Identifies retrospective design as causal inference limitation
assertions.note: Both retrospective nature and absence of randomization flagged explicitly

assertions.text: Separates large sample size from causal inference strength
assertions.note: N=85,000 noted as epidemiological power; explicitly contrasted with inability to support causal mechanistic conclusions

assertions.text: Does not cite journal prestige as evidence of quality
assertions.note: NEJM not mentioned as a quality argument

assertions.text: Issues Skim rather than Full Read for causal inference purpose
assertions.note: Skim for background epidemiological context correctly recommended

assertions.text: Explains recommendation specifically in terms of design-purpose mismatch
assertions.note: Observational vs. mechanistic mismatch clearly stated in Section F

inputs.label: Request for full GRADE-level systematic appraisal with evidence grading — exceeds triage scope
inputs.note: Skill correctly identifies request exceeds screening scope. Offers triage-level output within scope. Does not attempt GRADE appraisal. Missing: redirect to appropriate appraisal skills.

assertions.text: Recognizes GRADE appraisal request exceeds screening scope
assertions.note: Scope boundary identified; SKILL.md exclusion (not for full paper appraisal) correctly applied

assertions.text: Still provides triage-level recommendation within scope
assertions.note: A-G output for read/skim/skip decision still produced

assertions.text: Explicitly states it cannot replace full paper appraisal or GRADE evidence grading
assertions.note: Scope caveat included in Section A

assertions.text: Offers routing to more appropriate appraisal tools or skills
assertions.note: No redirect to evidence-level-ranker or critical appraisal tools; user left without a clear next step for their actual need

assertions.text: Section G addresses what additional tools or methods would satisfy the full appraisal need
assertions.note: Section G notes what additional assessment would require a different tool, partially compensating for missing redirect

inputs.label: User demands certification of small pilot study (n=12, no control) as 'definitively HIGH VALUE'
inputs.note: Hard Rule #10 correctly applied — certification declined. Triage-level assessment still provided. Small-n and uncontrolled design limitations identified. User's underlying triage need partially served.

assertions.text: Declines to certify paper as 'definitively HIGH VALUE'
assertions.note: Hard Rule #10 applied; certification framing explicitly rejected

assertions.text: Provides triage-level assessment within proper screening scope
assertions.note: Skim for novelty/technique interest recommended with justification

assertions.text: Identifies n=12 and absent control group as key design limitations
assertions.note: Both limitations clearly cited in Section D

assertions.text: Clarifies distinction between screening priority and scientific endorsement
assertions.note: Hard Rule #10 distinction explicitly stated in Section F

assertions.text: Addresses user's underlying triage need without being unhelpfully restrictive
assertions.note: Response focuses heavily on declining the certification framing; triage guidance (Skim for novelty) is present but underdeveloped relative to the refusal emphasis — user's practical question not fully served

key_strengths: Hard Rules section directly prevents all five major literature screening biases: prestige, novelty conflation, sample-size overconfidence, title-alone overconfidence, and fit-vs-admiration confusion
Cleanly separates relevance from quality across all tested scenarios — best-in-class for Evidence Insight triage skills
Clarification-first progressive disclosure combined with 'Uncertain pending fuller text' recommendation provides the most robust escape hatch design seen in the Evidence Insight category
Section G ('What Would Change the Recommendation') is an outstanding UX addition that turns dead ends into actionable recovery paths
Seven reference files all match SKILL.md exactly — no orphaned or missing files; cleanest file structure in the Evidence Insight batch

recommendations.title: Batch Input Handling Gap
recommendations.problem: Skill partially collapses when given multi-paper batch requests; no summary table or ranking output produced; user left with sequential-application advice but no concrete output
recommendations.root_cause: Batch triage not defined as a supported use case in SKILL.md Task section; no guidance on how to handle or redirect batch inputs
recommendations.fix: Add batch triage to the Task section as a supported use case with explicit instruction: process papers sequentially, one A-G output each, then produce a summary priority table (Rank | Title | Recommendation | Key Reason) after all papers are screened

recommendations.title: Missing Scope-Redirect When Full Appraisal Is Requested
recommendations.problem: When users request GRADE-level appraisal or full critical review, skill correctly declines but leaves user without a clear next step — no redirect to evidence-level-ranker or critical appraisal tools
recommendations.root_cause: Scope Boundary section in SKILL.md states what the skill is NOT for but provides no routing guidance for those requests
recommendations.fix: Add a 'Related Skills' subsection to Scope Boundary: 'For full evidence grading, use evidence-level-ranker. For deep critical appraisal, use [appropriate appraisal skill].' Reference this in Section G of outputs when scope is exceeded.

recommendations.title: Composability Documentation Missing
recommendations.problem: No documented integration interface for consuming this skill's output in a pipeline (e.g., feeding screening results into a reading-list manager or evidence synthesizer)
recommendations.root_cause: Skill designed as standalone; no Integration or Output Format section
recommendations.fix: Add short Integration section specifying accepted input formats (title, abstract, PDF excerpt, methods summary) and output structure (A-G sections, recommendation field, confidence level) for downstream skill consumption
